full_prompt: Design the spherical protective cage (drone soccer ball) for a drone-soccer 2024-25 rulebook benchmark. The cage is a spherical lattice of outside diameter 40 cm with a tolerance of plus or minus 2 cm (so 38 cm to 42 cm OD acceptable, 40 cm nominal). Inside the cage sits a racing quadcopter of approximately 250 g mass; the assembled ball including cage, motors, frame, battery, and props must still be compact enough to pass through a goal of inner diameter 60 cm at a height of 3-3.5 m above the arena floor. The arena is 14-20 m long, 7-10 m wide, and 4-5 m high. The cage must absorb repeated inter-ball collisions and ball-to-goal contacts during a match without permanent deformation. Specifically, the cage must survive a head-on collision between two balls traveling at 10 m/s each (relative 20 m/s closing speed, each ball approximately 500 g total) as a quasi-static 100 N impact equivalent applied at any point on the cage surface — no plastic yield, no fracture, and no intrusion of the cage geometry into the internal propeller disk (tip clearance to cage is typically 25-35 mm in standard 3-inch prop racing quads). The cage mass must be below 250 g (so that ball + drone total stays under 500 g). Typical cage construction is CFRP rods or molded carbon lattice. Primary structural pass/fail: (1) cage OD within 40 ± 2 cm; (2) propeller-to-cage tip clearance >= 20 mm at all points; (3) max cage rod stress under 100 N collision equivalent <= 0.67 x rod UTS (SF 1.5); (4) cage mass <= 250 g; (5) cage must recover elastically (no residual deformation > 2 mm after 100 N impact release). Primary verification is linear static structural FEA for the 100 N collision equivalent. Vibration (rotor-speed-induced) analysis is relevant to the inner drone but NOT required at the cage design gate. Thermal, fluid (prop aero), and radiation analyses are explicitly NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the cage, a mass budget, and a report listing cage OD in cm, cage mass in g, min prop-to-cage clearance in mm, max rod stress at 100 N in MPa, and residual deformation in mm.
short_prompt:
Design the spherical protective cage (the "ball") for a drone-soccer racing quadcopter per the 2024-25 Drone Soccer rulebook. The cage is a lightweight lattice (pultruded CFRP rod or molded carbon) supporting the 250 g drone inside through a 6-point internal interface, passing through a 60 cm goal. Cage + drone must stay under 500 g total.

Envelope: spherical, 40 +/- 2 cm outside diameter, minimum 20 mm prop-to-cage clearance everywhere.

Load case.
- LC1 collision: 100 N quasi-static equivalent (representing two 500 g balls closing at 20 m/s) applied at the worst-case point on the cage surface; cage must recover elastically (residual deformation <= 2 mm after release).

prompt.title: Drone-soccer 40 cm ball cage
prompt.brief: Design a 40 ± 2 cm spherical CFRP cage around a 250 g racing quad, cage mass ≤ 250 g, survives 100 N collision equivalent.

material.typical_cage: CFRP pultruded rod or molded lattice

load_cases.name: collision_100N
load_cases.description: quasi-static equiv of 20 m/s closing at 500 g per ball

boundary_conditions.inner_drone_frame: provides internal 6-point support to cage rods

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: total ball ≤ 500 g with 250 g drone

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Prop aero not in cage gate.
